  {"factor": "Ecological Impacts", "grade": "VAGUE", "evidence": "The lawsuits allege that the defendants' historical operations in oil and gas fields within the Louisiana onshore coastal zone failed to comply with state permits... The scope and scale of plaintiffs' damages demands are significant and unprecedented, including substantial remediation costs, natural resource (ecological impact) damages and the claimed costs for restoring coastal wetlands allegedly impacted by oil and gas field operations."},
  {"factor": "Human Rights & Community Relations", "grade": "VAGUE", "evidence": "The remaining proceedings arising from the Incident broadly seek penalties, costs, damages and compensation for alleged environmental, personal injury, health and economic harm as a result of the Incident."},
  {"factor": "Employee Health & Safety", "grade": "UNDISCLOSED", "evidence": null},
  {"factor": "Business Model Resilience", "grade": "VAGUE", "evidence": "BP p.l.c., BP America Inc. and BP Products North America Inc. are co-defendants with other oil and gas companies in approximately 32 lawsuits brought in various state and federal courts on behalf of various governmental and private parties. The lawsuits generally assert claims under a variety of legal theories seeking to hold the defendant companies responsible for impacts allegedly caused by and/or relating to climate change."},
  {"factor": "Business Ethics", "grade": "VAGUE", "evidence": "Underlying many of the legal theories are allegations regarding deceptive communication and disinformation to the public."},
  {"factor": "Management of the Legal & Regulatory Environment", "grade": "QUANTIFIED", "evidence": "BP p.l.c., BP America Inc. and BP Products North America Inc. are co-defendants with other oil and gas companies in approximately 32 lawsuits brought in various state and federal courts... Six coastal parishes and the State of Louisiana have filed over 40 separate lawsuits in state courts in Louisiana against various oil and gas companies seeking damages for coastal erosion. bp entities were named defendants in 17 of these cases... In April 2025, following a three-week trial, the jury returned a verdict against Chevron awarding plaintiffs $745 million."},
  {"factor": "Critical Incident Risk Management", "grade": "VAGUE", "evidence": "BPXP was lease operator of Mississippi Canyon, Block 252 in the Gulf of America, where the semi-submersible rig Deepwater Horizon was deployed at the time of the 20 April 2010 explosion and fire and resulting oil spill (the Incident)... Any outstanding Deepwater Horizon related claims are not expected to have a material impact on the group's financial performance."}
